ModuleName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics, covering the definition and purpose of data analytics, the four types of analytics (descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive), and the phases of the CRISP-DM framework; used by instructors to plan lessons and by learners to navigate the module's scope.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: This page outlines the key topics covered in the module — including definitions of data analytics, its role in organizational decision-making, and the stages of the analytics lifecycle — serving as a navigational overview that helps learners and instructors understand what concepts will be taught and in what order.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction_to_Data_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing data analytics and the analytics lifecycle, explaining what data analytics is, how organizations derive competitive advantage from insights, the four analytics types, and the CRISP-DM process; used by instructors to deliver lectures and by learners to review core concepts.

Artifacts.Name: What is Data Analytics?
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces data analytics by explaining what it is, why organizations collect and analyze data, and how turning raw information into actionable insights creates business value — serving as a foundational orientation for learners new to the field.

Artifacts.Name: The Four Types of Analytics
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the four types of analytics — descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive — by defining what question each answers and how businesses use each type to progress from understanding past events to guiding future decisions.

Artifacts.Name: The CRISP-DM Process Framework
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers the CRISP-DM framework by walking learners through its six phases — from business understanding to deployment — giving instructors and learners a shared process model for structuring real-world data analytics projects.

Artifacts.Name: Real-World Business Applications
Artifacts.Description: This reading surveys how data analytics is applied across multiple industries, providing concrete examples of descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics in practice so learners can connect theoretical concepts to real organizational problems.

Artifacts.Name: Foundational Case Studies
Artifacts.Description: This reading presents foundational case studies from industries such as retail, healthcare, finance, sports, and e-commerce to illustrate how organizations shifted from intuition-based to data-driven decision-making, helping learners contextualize core analytics concepts.

Artifacts.Name: Foundational Case Studies
Artifacts.Description: This reading presents foundational case studies from industries such as retail, healthcare, finance, sports, and e-commerce to illustrate how organizations shifted from intuition-based to data-driven decision-making, helping learners contextualize core analytics concepts.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank assessing learners' understanding of key module concepts, including the definition of data analytics, the distinction between analytics types, and CRISP-DM phases, with scenario-based questions; used by instructors to build graded assessments or by learners for self-testing.

Artifacts.Name: Case Study Analysis: Data Analytics in Real Business Practice
Artifacts.Description: An individual case study assignment (2–4 hours) in which learners analyse real business scenarios to apply data analytics concepts and the analytics lifecycle, using the TILT framework to understand the purpose, task, and criteria before submitting their analysis as directed by the instructor.
